title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Styles foundational professional reporting: - Define , reference many times — consistency across document - Easy update: change one style definition references automatically pick change - Composable: combine base styles (bold, red text) complex styles specific use cases ksTFL uses named style system: define styles add_style() giving id, reference name wherever need (labelStyleRef, valueStyleRef, labelStyleRef stubs, etc.).

previous_headings: 10 — Styles and f_combine() for combined style references
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Create atomic styles focus one aspect (font, alignment, color):
code: # Create a table spec spec_base_styles <- create_table(data = demog_tbl, cols = c(subject_id, age, sex, trt))  # Define reusable base styles spec_base_styles <- add_style(spec_base_styles, id = "bold_header",                               s_font(bold = TRUE, font_size = "12pt"))  spec_base_styles <- add_style(spec_base_styles, id = "right_align",                               s_paragraph(alignment = "right"))  spec_base_styles <- add_style(spec_base_styles, id = "light_gray_bg",                               s_table_style(background_color = "#F5F5F5"))  # Apply to columns spec_base_styles <- define_cols(spec_base_styles,                                 c(subject_id, age, sex, trt),                                 label = c("Subject ID", "Age", "Sex", "Treatment"),                                 labelStyleRef = c("bold_header", "", "", ""))  print(spec_base_styles) # close example chunk

previous_headings: 10 — Styles and f_combine() for combined style references
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Apply multiple styles single element using f_combine() — merge render time:  f_combine() works: - Takes multiple style names arguments - Returns reference object tells package merge styles - Order matters last-win conflict resolution (later arguments override earlier ones)
code: # Create spec and define base styles spec_combined <- create_table(data = demog_tbl, cols = c(subject_id, age, sex, trt))  # Define atomic styles spec_combined <- add_style(spec_combined, id = "bold_text", s_font(bold = TRUE)) spec_combined <- add_style(spec_combined, id = "red_color", s_font(color = "#CC0000")) spec_combined <- add_style(spec_combined, id = "centered", s_paragraph(alignment = "center"))  # Combine styles: bold + red text + centered spec_combined <- define_cols(spec_combined,                              subject_id,                              label = "Subject ID",                              labelStyleRef = f_combine("bold_text", "red_color", "centered")) |>  define_cols(c(sex, trt),             label=c('Sex', 'Treatment'),             labelStyleRef = f_combine('b','i'),             valueStyleRef = f_combine('ar', 'i')             ) |> # Different columns can use different combinations define_cols(age, label = "Age",             labelStyleRef = f_combine("bold_text", "centered"),             valueStyleRef = 'ac')

title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Overview: define_cols() sets properties globally rows, compute_cols() applies conditional actions specific rows matching condition. Common use cases: - Style rows specific value occurs (e.g., first/last occurrence, threshold-based) - Merge columns certain rows (e.g., group headers) - Insert separator summary rows programmatically

title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Apply style columns rows matching condition:
code: # Sample data with groups data <- data.frame(   group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),   metric = c("Value 1", "Value 2", "Value 3", "Value 4", "Value 5"),   count = c(10, 20, 15, 25, 30) )  # Create spec and define styles spec <- create_table(data) |>   add_style(id = "group_header", s_font(bold = TRUE, color = "#0000FF")) |>   add_style(id = "emphasize",    s_table_style(background_color = "#FFFFCC"))  # Apply conditional styling: bold+blue for first occurrence of each group spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(firstOf(group), c_style(c(metric, count), styleRef = "group_header"))  # Apply conditional styling: highlight rows with high count spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(count > 20, c_style(count, styleRef = "emphasize"))

what: Example 2: Combining styles in conditional rows
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Apply multiple styles single column using f_combine():
code: spec <- create_table(data) |>   add_style(id = "bold",      s_font(bold = TRUE)) |>   add_style(id = "large",     s_font(font_size = "14pt")) |>   add_style(id = "highlight", s_table_style(background_color = "#FFFF00"))  # Apply combined styles to first group occurrence spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(firstOf(group),      c_style(metric, styleRef = f_combine("bold", "large", "highlight"))   )

what: Example 3: Column merging in conditional rows
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Merge adjacent columns rows matching condition:
code: spec <- create_table(data) |>   add_style(id = "group_label", s_table_style(background_color = "#D9D9D9"))  # Merge metric and count columns for first occurrence (group header style) spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(firstOf(group),      c_merge(c(metric, count), styleRef = "group_label")   )

title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Insert empty rows (separators) rows content another column:
code: spec <- create_table(data) |>   add_style(id = "separator", s_table_style(background_color = "#E8E8E8"))  # Insert empty separator above first group occurrence spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(firstOf(group),      c_addrow(pos = "above")  # Empty row, no value_from   )  # Insert summary row below last group occurrence (using a specific column as content) spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(lastOf(group),      c_addrow(pos = "below", value_from = "group", styleRef = "separator")   )

title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Force page break group ends (useful long groups):
code: spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(lastOf(group),     c_pageBreak()   )

what: Example 5: Multiple actions on same rows
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Combine styling, merging, row insertion single compute_cols() call:  Key concepts: - Conditions unevaluated expressions evaluated report generation time (create_report()) - Helper functions (firstOf(), lastOf(), firstRow(), lastRow(), rowNumber(), everyNth(), firstOfBlock()) available inside compute_cols() conditions — standalone exported functions. See vignette("Advanced_StyleRows") full details. - Multiple calls accumulate: calling compute_cols() multiple times spec appends actions - Multiple actions one call: row can styling, merging, row insertion simultaneously - Overlapping conditions: multiple conditions match row, actions apply (styling aggregates, merging rules apply) - value_from: Optional c_addrow() — omit empty separator rows - Performance: Conditions evaluated per row report assembly; style consolidation happens automatically
code: spec <- create_table(data) |>   add_style(id = "header",    s_font(bold = TRUE)) |>   add_style(id = "separator", s_table_style(background_color = "#E8E8E8"))  # For first group occurrence: add separator, style, and merge spec <- spec |>   compute_cols(firstOf(group),      c_addrow(pos = "above"),  # Add empty separator     c_style(metric, styleRef = "header"),  # Style metric column     c_merge(c(metric, count))  # Merge adjacent columns   )

title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: write_doc() primary output function. combines two lower-level steps one: Validates serializes report JSON, writing metadata data files metaPath Renders JSON DOCX file outDir
code: create_report() → write_doc()  →  output.docx

what: Example: Assemble and render a multi-spec report
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Tip: Use tfl_list_templates() discover available template names (e.g., "Navy_Pro", "Carbon_Dark"). Use run_styles_editor() interactively preview customize templates. workflow: write_doc(..., overrideTemplate = NULL) keeps per-spec docTemplate styling. write_doc(..., overrideTemplate = <name_or_path>) applies single global template specs. Key parameters: - report: TFL_report object (created via create_report()) - name: Base name output DOCX (e.g. "example_report" → example_report.docx) - outDir: Directory output DOCX written - metaPath: Directory intermediate JSON data files written Advanced: need inspect JSON rendering (e.g. debugging CI pipelines), can split call save_report() + replay_report(). See ?write_doc ?replay_report details.
code: # Create multiple specs table_spec <- create_table(data = labs_tbl, cols = c(subject_id, ALT, AST)) table_spec <- add_title(table_spec, "Laboratory Results") table_spec <- set_page_style(table_spec, docTemplate = "Navy_Pro")  text_spec <- create_text() text_spec <- add_body_text(text_spec, "All values are from the locked database.") text_spec <- set_page_style(text_spec, docTemplate = "Carbon_Dark")  # Assemble into report report_full <- create_report(table_spec, text_spec)  # A) Per-spec templates (default): each spec uses its own docTemplate write_doc(report_full, name = "example_report", outDir = "./out", metaPath = tempdir())  # B) Global override: force one template for all specs write_doc(   report_full,   name = "example_report_global_override",   outDir = "./out",   metaPath = tempdir(),   overrideTemplate = system.file("templates", "Navy_Pro.json", package = "ksTFL", mustWork = TRUE) )

title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: real clinical reporting workflow, many tables/listings share: - Common headers (study name, database version, confidentiality notice) - Common footers (company name, page numbers, disclaimers) - Default body text (standard disclaimers methodology notes) Instead adding every spec, use tfl_set_options() set session defaults . specs created afterward inherit settings.

previous_headings: 13 — Session-wide tfl_options: common headers/footers and default body text
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Key point: Headers footers tfl_set_options() automatically applied new specs. need call add_header() .
code: # Set session defaults (applies to all NEW specs created after this call) tfl_set_options(   add_header("Study ABC", "Phase II Safety Study", "CONFIDENTIAL"),   add_footer("Company Confidential", "Page {PAGE} of {NUMPAGES}") )  # Create spec — automatically inherits headers/footers from options spec_with_opts <- create_table(data = demog_tbl, cols = c(subject_id, age, sex, trt)) spec_with_opts <- add_title(spec_with_opts, "Demographics Table")  print(spec_with_opts) # close example chunk

what: Example 2: Override session options in a specific spec
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: can override session defaults individual specs: Rule: Spec-level settings always take precedence session defaults (last-win strategy).
code: # Session options are still in effect from previous example  # Create a spec with session defaults spec_default <- create_table(data = labs_tbl, cols = c(subject_id, ALT, AST)) spec_default <- add_title(spec_default, "Lab Results (using session defaults)")  # Create another spec but override the header spec_override <- create_table(data = demog_tbl, cols = c(subject_id, age)) spec_override <- add_header(spec_override, "Study XYZ", "Different Study", "CONFIDENTIAL")  # Overrides session default spec_override <- add_title(spec_override, "Demographics (custom header)")  print(spec_default)   # Uses session header from tfl_set_options print(spec_override)  # Uses custom header from add_header call # close example chunk

what: Example 3: Check and reset session options
title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: Inspect current session options reset defaults:
code: # Check current options current_options <- tfl_get_options() str(current_options)  # Get a single option current_missings <- tfl_get_option("missings") cat("Current missings representation:", current_missings, "\n")  # Reset to package defaults tfl_reset_options()  # Now new specs will use package defaults instead of your custom session options spec_reset <- create_table(data = demog_tbl, cols = c(subject_id, age)) print(spec_reset) # close example chunk

title: Reporting Examples using ksTFL
text: comprehensive vignette demonstrates complete ksTFL reporting pipeline using exported helpers. Examples covered: 1. Minimal table/figure/text creation (single-spec workflows) 2. Define columns: Single column, batch updates, parameter recycling (NEW) 3. Column widths: Auto-calculation, locking, mixed units (NEW) 4. Multi-level tables titles, subtitles, footnotes 5. Combining multiple specs report 6. Spanning headers (stub columns) styling 7. Named styles combining f_combine() (expanded) 8. End--end render write_doc() 9. Session defaults tfl_set_options() Key patterns remember: - Batch operations: Use c() select multiple columns, provide single values (recycled) N values (one--one mapping) - Multiple calls: Chain define_cols(), add_style(), etc. — call merges previous settings - Flexible column widths: Auto-calculated default; lock specific columns colWidth trigger recalculation others - Combining styles: Use f_combine() --fly combinations; define named styles reuse - Session defaults: Use tfl_set_options() ; new specs inherit settings (override per-spec needed) Running examples locally: Set top chunk eval=TRUE generate sample data, execute examples order. code uses exported functions — internal API manipulation needed. details function parameters, see roxygen documentation: ?create_table, ?define_cols, ?add_style, ?write_doc, etc.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: guide covers complete styling system ksTFL: - Style primitives (s_* helpers) fonts, paragraphs, spacing, indentation, tables, borders - Declaring named styles add_style() - Referencing combining styles style references f_combine() - Applying styles columns, labels, stubs, content - Best practices maintainable, reusable style systems runnable reporting examples integrating styles see Reporting Examples. quick start see Getting Started.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: styling-system vignette. Audience: users defining reusable style systems teams studies Focus: style primitives, composition, references, maintainable conventions Outcome: consistent style architecture across many reports

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Professional clinical documents require: - Consistency: headers look , numeric columns align right, subtitles use font - Maintainability: Change one style definition, references update automatically - Composability: Build complex styles simple building blocks (font + paragraph alignment + table background) - Separation concerns: Define styles , apply many times without repeating details ksTFL uses named style system: declare styles add_style() giving id, reference name needed. Styles composable (f_combine()) consolidated automatically build reports.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Define atomic styles first: Create small, focused styles (bold headers, right-aligned text, light gray background) Reference name: define_cols(), add_title(), add_span_header() (tidyselect support), use labelStyleRef valueStyleRef point styles id Combine needed: Use f_combine() merge multiple styles --fly ad-hoc combinations Let create_report() consolidate: report builder automatically merges combined styles renderer receives clean, consolidated styles Never inspect internal fields: Don’t manually look spec$attribs$styles — let package manage consolidation

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: style primitives begin s_ must used inside add_style(). helpers validate parameter names values, raising informative errors used incorrectly.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Control font appearance (name, size, weight, color, decorations). Parameters: - font_name: Font family (e.g., “Arial”, “Courier New”, “Times New Roman”, “Georgia”) - font_size: Size units (e.g., “12pt”, “11pt”) - bold: Logical (TRUE/FALSE) - italic: Logical (TRUE/FALSE) - underline: Logical (TRUE/FALSE) - color: Color hex code (e.g., “#000000”, “#FF0000”) color name (e.g., “red”, “black”, “blue”) - highlight: Background highlight color hex code color name Example:
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # Bold, 12pt Arial, red text (using hex code) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "header_bold_red",   s_font(font_name = "Arial", font_size = "12pt", bold = TRUE, color = "#CC0000"))  # Underlined, 10pt monospace, blue text (using color name) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "code_style",   s_font(font_name = "Courier New", font_size = "10pt", underline = TRUE, color = "blue"))  # Yellow highlight with black text spec <- add_style(spec, id = "highlighted",   s_font(color = "black", highlight = "yellow"))

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Control text alignment, spacing /, line spacing, indentation. Parameters: - alignment: Text alignment — “left”, “right”, “center”, “justify”, “distributed” - spacing: Spacing /(use s_spacing(...)) - indents: Left/right indentation, first-line indent (use s_indents(...)) Example:
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # Centered text with 6pt spacing before/after spec <- add_style(spec, id = "centered_spaced",   s_paragraph(alignment = "center",                spacing = s_spacing(before = "6pt", after = "6pt")))  # Right-aligned with left indent spec <- add_style(spec, id = "right_indented",   s_paragraph(alignment = "right",               indents = s_indents(left = "10pt")))

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Controls spacing paragraph, paragraph, lines. Parameters: - : Space paragraph (e.g., “6pt”, “12pt”) - : Space paragraph (e.g., “6pt”, “12pt”) - line_spacing: Line spacing multiplier (e.g., 1.0, 1.5, 2.0 single/1.5-line/double spacing) Note: s_spacing() always used inside s_paragraph(), never standalone. Example:
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # Double-spaced with 12pt spacing after each paragraph spec <- add_style(spec, id = "double_spaced",   s_paragraph(spacing = s_spacing(after = "12pt", line_spacing = 2.0)))

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Controls left/right margins first-line indentation within paragraph. Parameters: - left: Left indent (e.g., “10pt”, “1cm”) - right: Right indent (e.g., “10pt”) - first_line: First-line indent (e.g., “20pt” hanging indent) Note: s_indents() always used inside s_paragraph(), never standalone. Example:
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # Hanging indent (first line outdented, rest indented) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "hanging_indent",   s_paragraph(indents = s_indents(left = "20pt", first_line = "-20pt")))  # Left and right margins with left indent spec <- add_style(spec, id = "block_indent",   s_paragraph(indents = s_indents(left = "30pt", right = "30pt")))

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Control cell background, row height, vertical alignment, text orientation, borders. Parameters: - background_color: Cell background color (hex, e.g., “#E8E8E8”) - row_height: Height table row (e.g., “25pt”) - topEmptyLine: Optional empty spacer row header (e.g., “6pt”, use NULL 0pt disable) - bottomEmptyLine: Optional empty spacer row bottom border (e.g., “6pt”, use NULL 0pt disable) - vertical_alignment: “top”, “center”, “bottom” - text_orientation: “horizontal”, “vertical_90”, “vertical_270” - borders: Border specification (use s_borders(...)) Example:
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # Light gray background, centered vertically, fixed row height spec <- add_style(spec, id = "header_cell",   s_table_style(background_color = "#F2F2F2",                 row_height = "30pt",                 vertical_alignment = "center"))  # Vertically rotated text (90 degrees) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "rotated_header",   s_table_style(text_orientation = "vertical_90"))  # Add table-level spacer rows via set_document() spec <- set_document(   spec,   topEmptyLine = "6pt",   bottomEmptyLine = "6pt" )

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Define borders four sides cell. side takes s_border() line style, width, color. Parameters (side): - top: Top border (use s_border(...)) - bottom: Bottom border (use s_border(...)) - left: Left border (use s_border(...)) - right: Right border (use s_border(...)) Note: s_borders() always used inside s_table_style(), never standalone.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Defines single border line style, width, color. Parameters: - color: Color hex code (e.g., “#000000”) color name (e.g., “black”, “red”) - width: Line width (e.g., “1pt”, “2pt”, “0.5pt”) - line_style: “single”, “double”, “dashed”, “dotted”, “thick”, “none” Example:
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # All borders: thin single lines in dark gray spec <- add_style(spec, id = "all_borders",   s_table_style(     borders = s_borders(       top = s_border(color = "grey40", width = "1pt", line_style = "single"),       bottom = s_border(color = "grey40", width = "1pt", line_style = "single"),       left = s_border(color = "grey40", width = "1pt", line_style = "single"),       right = s_border(color = "grey40", width = "1pt", line_style = "single")     )   ) )  # Heavy bottom border in dark color (using hex code) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "bottom_border_heavy",   s_table_style(     borders = s_borders(       bottom = s_border(color = "#333333", width = "2pt", line_style = "thick")     )   ) )

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Named styles foundation ksTFL styling system. style unique id contains one style primitives.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Parameters: - spec: TFL_spec object - id: Unique name style (e.g., “header_bold”, “numeric_right”) - ...: One style primitives (s_font(), s_paragraph(), s_table_style(), etc.)
code: spec <- add_style(spec, id = "style_name",   s_font(...),   s_paragraph(...),   s_table_style(...) )

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # Comprehensive header style: bold white text on gray background, centered spec <- add_style(spec, id = "table_header",   s_font(font_name = "Arial", font_size = "12pt", bold = TRUE, color = "#FFFFFF"),   s_paragraph(alignment = "center", spacing = s_spacing(before = "6pt", after = "6pt")),   s_table_style(background_color = "#333333", row_height = "30pt", vertical_alignment = "center") )  # Simple numeric style: right-aligned spec <- add_style(spec, id = "numeric_right",   s_paragraph(alignment = "right") )  # ID/key style: bold spec <- add_style(spec, id = "id_bold",   s_font(bold = TRUE) )

what: Multiple calls merge with last-win strategy
title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Calling add_style() multiple times id merges styles (last-win semantics):
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # First call: defines font spec <- add_style(spec, id = "emphasis", s_font(bold = TRUE))  # Second call: adds paragraph alignment; bold is preserved spec <- add_style(spec, id = "emphasis", s_paragraph(alignment = "center"))  # Result: "emphasis" has both bold font AND center alignment

text: f_combine() lets apply multiple styles single element without pre-defining combined style.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  spec <- add_style(spec, id = "bold", s_font(bold = TRUE)) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "red", s_font(color = "red"))  # Using color name spec <- add_style(spec, id = "centered", s_paragraph(alignment = "center"))  # Apply bold + red + centered to a column header spec <- define_cols(spec, mpg, label = "MPG",   labelStyleRef = f_combine("bold", "red", "centered"))

code: spec <- create_table(mtcars)  # Define base styles spec <- add_style(spec, id = "bold", s_font(bold = TRUE)) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "italic", s_font(italic = TRUE)) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "centered", s_paragraph(alignment = "center")) spec <- add_style(spec, id = "right", s_paragraph(alignment = "right"))  # Apply different combinations per column spec <- define_cols(spec, c(mpg, cyl, hp),   label = c("MPG", "Cylinders", "HP"),   labelStyleRef = c(     f_combine("bold", "centered"),      # mpg: bold + centered     f_combine("italic", "right"),       # cyl: italic + right     f_combine("bold", "italic", "centered")  # hp: bold + italic + centered   ) )

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Problem: used f_combine(), must call create_report() styles consolidated.
code: spec <- create_table(mtcars) spec <- define_cols(spec, mpg, labelStyleRef = f_combine("bold", "red"))  # CORRECT: create_report() consolidates combined styles before rendering report <- create_report(spec)  # Merges "bold" + "red" into a single resolved style write_doc(report, name = "out", outDir = "./output", metaPath = tempdir())

Generates unique hash-based names consolidated styles 5. Updates references point consolidated style don’t need inspect manipulate spec$attribs$styles — consolidation automatic transparent.

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: ksTFL ships library single-property style atoms accessible via f_combine() directly styleRef values. atom sets exactly one visual property; compose freely f_combine(). Discovering atoms programmatically: Use tfl_print_style_atoms() (alias tfl_style_atoms_catalog()) print available atoms grouped category colour-coded output console:
code: # Print all built-in atoms categorised and colour-coded tfl_print_style_atoms()

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: Usage:
code: # Single atom as styleRef spec <- add_footnote(spec, "Source: database.", styleRef = "tw_80")  # Combine atoms with f_combine() spec <- define_cols(spec, value,   valueStyleRef = f_combine("font_verdana", "ar", "fs_9"))  # Combine atoms with named styles spec <- add_style(spec, id = "group_hdr",   s_font(bold = TRUE),   s_paragraph(spacing = s_spacing(before = "4pt"))) spec <- compute_cols(spec, firstOf(group),   c_style(everything(), styleRef = f_combine("group_hdr", "bg_gray")))

title: Styling Guide (ksTFL)
text: table narrower full content width, titles, subtitles, footnotes, body text span full page width default — wider table . tw_* atoms apply symmetric left right paragraph indentation text blocks visually align table edges. Indent values calculated A4 landscape 0.5 left/right page margins (content width ≈ 27.16 cm). 5 % step corresponds 0.68 cm per side. Usage — match footnotes titles narrower table: Combine atoms using f_combine(): Apply via named style reuse across multiple specs: Note: indent values assume default A4 landscape page 0.5 margins. use different page size, orientation, margins via set_page_style(), calculate indents s_indents(left = ..., right = ...) inside add_style().
code: spec <- create_table(data) |>   set_document(contentWidth = "80%") |>   add_title("Demographics Table",   styleRef = "tw_80") |>   add_subtitle("Safety Population", styleRef = "tw_80") |>   add_footnote("Source: study database.", styleRef = "tw_80") # Bold title, indented to match a 75 % table spec <- add_title(spec, "Efficacy Summary",                   styleRef = f_combine("b", "tw_75"))  # Small italic footnote, indented to match a 70 % table spec <- add_footnote(spec, "Values are least-squares means.",                      styleRef = f_combine("i", "fs_8", "tw_70")) # Define the style on each spec (or use a helper function to apply it) add_footnote_80 <- function(spec, text) {   spec <- add_style(spec, id = "fn_80",     s_font(font_size = "8pt", italic = TRUE),     s_paragraph(alignment = "left",                 indents = s_indents(left = "2.72cm", right = "2.72cm")))   add_footnote(spec, text, styleRef = "fn_80") }  spec1 <- add_footnote_80(spec1, "a. p < 0.05") spec2 <- add_footnote_80(spec2, "b. LOCF imputation")

title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: ksTFL professional R package designed fill long-standing gap R ecosystem: lack dedicated, end--end solution producing well-formatted, regulatory-compliant clinical Tables, Figures, Listings (TFLs). R excels statistical analysis, generating submission-quality DOCX outputs meet pharmaceutical industry standards traditionally required fragile workarounds external tooling. ksTFL solves distilling best ideas existing reporting solutions simple, minimalistic, yet highly flexible declarative language — compact set composable functions whose combinations can produce virtually clinical output format. core philosophy data–presentation separation: input data stays clean planar, free reporting artefacts merged cells, indentation columns, display-rows. Formatting, pagination, grouping, styling declared independently applied render time. keeps datasets maintainable, traceable, validation-ready — exactly regulated environments demand. hood, built-rendering engine text shaping converts declarative specs styled DOCX documents deterministic, pixel-perfect pagination. Rendering extremely fast even large datasets, making ksTFL practical batch production hundreds outputs single pipeline run.

title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: Separation Concerns: Metadata generation (R) decoupled document rendering; input data remain clean analysis-ready Declarative Syntax: Users describe render, render — small function vocabulary covers full range clinical outputs Deterministic Pagination: font shaping guarantees pixel-perfect, reproducible layouts pagination High Performance: C++ engine renders large multi-spec reports seconds Type Safety: Comprehensive input validation informative error messages Reproducibility: Specifications serializable. allows store metadata replay without re-runnig whole code

library(ksTFL) # 1. Initialize a table specification spec <- create_table(mtcars, cols = c(cyl, mpg, hp, wt)) # 2. Add document content spec <- spec |> add_title("Motor Trend Car Road Tests", styleRef = "i") |> add_title("Performance Metrics by Cylinder Count", styleRef = 'i') |> add_subtitle("Number of Cylinders: #ByGroup1", styleRef = f_combine('fc_blue','tw_50')) |> add_footnote("Source: 1974 Motor Trend US magazine", styleRef = 'tw_50') # 3. Define column properties spec <- spec |> define_cols(c(mpg, hp, wt), label = c("Miles/(US) gallon", "Horsepower", "Weight
(1000 lbs)"), valueStyleRef = 'ac' ) |> define_cols(cyl, isGrouping = TRUE, isVisible = FALSE) # 4. Apply conditional row styling spec <- spec |> compute_cols(hp > 200, c_style(hp, styleRef = "fc_red")) spec <- spec |> set_document(contentWidth = '50%') # 5. Create report and render to DOCX report <- create_report(spec)

title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: Combine specifications reports render DOCX: create_report() Features: Consolidates styles across specs (deduplicates merged styles) Validates style references exist Assigns sequential document order Generates unique data references Evaluates deferred compute_cols() conditions data environment Supports mixing TFL_spec TFL_report objects save_report() Parameters: report: TFL_report object create_report() docFileName: Output filename (e.g., "report.docx") outDir: Output directory (defaults tfl_options) metaPath: Directory metadata/data files (defaults tempdir()) prettify: TRUE, formats JSON output debugging write_doc() Parameters: report: TFL_report object create_report() name: Base output filename (without .docx) outDir: Output directory final DOCX metaPath: Directory metadata/data files overrideTemplate: Optional global template override (name JSON path) font_dirs: Additional font search directories (optional) fallback_font: Custom fallback font path (optional) verbose: Print progress messages (default: FALSE)

title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: ksTFL ships 100+ atomic styles — single-property building blocks can used directly styleRef values combined f_combine(). atom sets exactly one visual property, making styles composable predictable. Use tfl_print_style_atoms() print avaialbe atomic styles, use addin call function. Usage: pass atom IDs directly styleRef parameter, combine multiple atoms: Font — Decoration Font — Family Font — Size (fs_7 fs_11): fs_7, fs_8, fs_9, fs_10, fs_11 (7–11 pt) Font — Color Text Highlight (background shading behind text) Paragraph — Alignment Paragraph — Indentation (0.5 cm steps): ind0–ind4 (left), rind0–rind4 (right). Aliases: indent_0–indent_4, rindent_0–rindent_4. Paragraph — Table-Width Shrink (tw_95–tw_50 5% steps): symmetric left+right indent align titles/footnotes/body text narrower table. Calculated A4 landscape 0.5 margins. Paragraph — Spacing: sp_0 (0 pt), sp_2 (2 pt), sp_4 (4 pt) — , line spacing 1.0. Paragraph — Pagination: kl (keep lines together page), kn (keep next row). Cell — Vertical Alignment: va_t / va_top, va_m / va_center, va_b / va_bottom. Cell — Text Orientation: to_h / text_horizontal, to_90 / text_vertical_90 (bottom--top), to_270 / text_vertical_270 (top--bottom). Cell — Background Color Row Height (spacer rows): row_h2 (2 pt), row_h4 (4 pt), row_h6 (6 pt). Borders: bt, bb, bl, br (1 pt black, single line); bt_th, bb_th (0.5 pt thin); bc_gray/bc_grey (color override gray), bc_white (suppress borders). Group Header Composites: grp_hdr (bold + indent reset + 4 pt space-), grp_hdr_i (bold + italic + indent reset + 4 pt space-).
code: # Single atom spec <- add_title(spec, "Demographics", styleRef = "b")  # Combine atoms with f_combine() spec <- define_cols(spec, mpg,   # `f_combine` used to merge atomic style to build the complex style on the fly   valueStyleRef = f_combine("font_verdana", "fs_10", "ar"))  spec <- add_footnote(spec, "Source: study database.",   styleRef = f_combine("i", "fc_gray", "tw_80")) # Compose atoms for a styled separator row spec <- compute_cols(spec, lastOf(category),   c_addrow("below", category,            styleRef = f_combine("row_h2", "bb_th", "bc_gray")))  # Group header with bold + space above spec <- compute_cols(spec, firstOf(param),   c_addrow("above", param, styleRef = "grp_hdr"))

title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: ksTFL supports HTML-like inline markup tags text content — cell values, column labels, titles, subtitles, footnotes, headers, footers, body text. Tags parsed C++ rendering engine converted styled OOXML runs output DOCX. Supported Tags: Key Characteristics: Nesting: fully supported — <>bold italic Case-insensitive: , , work attributes: tags carry attributes; use style system colors, fonts, etc. Unknown tags ignored: unrecognized tags silently stripped, content preserved Priority: inline markup overrides style-system settings (template, column, row styles) Common Patterns: Note: mutually exclusive — nested, innermost tag takes effect.
code:
# Multi-line column headers define_cols(spec, c(drug, placebo, total), label = c("DrugX
(N=160)", "Placebo
(N=158)", "Total
(N=318)")) # Superscripts in footnotes add_footnote(spec, "* p<0.05; † adjusted for baseline") # Bold key values in titles add_title(spec, "Table 14.1: Demographics and Baseline Characteristics") # Subscript in chemical formulas add_body_text(spec, "Concentration of CO2 measured in ppm") # Nested formatting add_footnote(spec, "Note: All values are least-squares means") # Paragraph breaks in body text add_body_text(spec, "Section 1 summary.

Section 2 begins here.")

title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
code: 1. Specification Creation    └─> create_table() / create_figure() / create_text()        └─> Returns TFL_spec with initialized structure  2. Content & Styling    └─> add_title(), add_subtitle(), add_footnote()    └─> define_cols() — column configuration    └─> add_style() — define named styles    └─> compute_cols() — conditional row styling    └─> set_document() — document metadata    └─> set_page_style() — page layout & template  3. Report Assembly    └─> create_report() — combines multiple specs        └─> Style consolidation & validation        └─> Sequential ordering & data references        └─> Deferred condition evaluation  4. Export    └─> save_report() — JSON spec + data files to disk  5. Rendering   └─> write_doc() / replay_report() — C++ engine produces styled .docx        └─> HarfBuzz text shaping → deterministic pagination        └─> OOXML emission → valid .docx (ZIP) package

what: Why proprietary fonts are not bundled
title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: Arial, Times New Roman, Courier New, Georgia, Verdana, Trebuchet MS proprietary fonts owned Microsoft /Monotype. license prohibits redistribution inside open-source packages. ksTFL therefore bundles Liberation family (SIL OFL 1.1) metrically compatible fallbacks. pixel-perfect output matching corporate templates, install original Microsoft fonts host system.

what: Installing Microsoft core fonts on Linux
title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: ttf-mscorefonts-installer package downloads installs Microsoft TrueType core fonts (Arial, Times New Roman, Courier New, Georgia, Verdana, Trebuchet MS, others) SourceForge. available Linux distribution repositories. Debian / Ubuntu: Fedora / RHEL / CentOS (via RPM Fusion manual SPEC): openSUSE: Arch Linux: installing, restart R (call tfl_rescan_fonts() running session) ksTFL picks new fonts.
code: sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install -y ttf-mscorefonts-installer sudo fc-cache -f # Fedora (requires RPM Fusion free repository to be enabled) sudo dnf install -y curl cabextract xorg-x11-font-utils fontconfig sudo rpm -i https://downloads.sourceforge.net/project/mscorefonts2/rpms/msttcore-fonts-installer-2.6-1.noarch.rpm sudo fc-cache -f sudo zypper install -y fetchmsttfonts sudo fc-cache -f # Available from the AUR yay -S ttf-ms-fonts sudo fc-cache -f

title: Framework for Clinical Tables, Figures, and Listings
text: Rocker images Debian-based, ttf-mscorefonts-installer can added directly. Include following Dockerfile: Tip: debconf-set-selections line automatically accepts Microsoft EULA, required non-interactive installs (CI/CD pipelines, Docker builds).
code: FROM rocker/r-ver:4.4  # Accept the Microsoft EULA non-interactively and install core fonts RUN echo "ttf-mscorefonts-installer msttcorefonts/accepted-mscorefonts-eula select true" \       | debconf-set-selections \     && apt-get update \     && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ttf-mscorefonts-installer fontconfig \     && fc-cache -f \     && apt-get clean \     && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

title: Concatenate a Value to Cell Text in Conditional Rows — c_glue
text: Must called inside compute_cols(). Constraints: Exactly one glue_col text must provided. cols resolves visible report columns via tidyselect. glue_col can reference data column, including hidden ones. glue_col must overlap cols. Behavior: glue value (glue_col text) empty NA, action silently skipped row/cell. target cell suppressed deduplication (dedupe = TRUE column), glue silently skipped preserve visual suppression repeated values. target cell suppressed concurrent c_merge() action (.e., non-leader merged cell), glue silently skipped. merge leader cell glued normally c_glue() targets column involved c_merge() first column. Multiple c_glue() calls column accumulate call order. Interaction actions: c_style(): Fully compatible — styling text modification independent. c_merge(): Compatible. Glue processed merge renderer. Non-leader (suppressed) merge cells skipped; merge-leader cell glued normally. c_addrow(): Fully compatible (affects different rows/cells). c_pageBreak(): Fully compatible.

Invisible columns participate width recalculation; visible columns included. set colWidth invisible columns (error raised attempted). isGrouping Whether column defines groups (length 1 length cols; NA skips position) isPaging Whether column defines pages (length 1 length cols; NA skips position) labelStyleRef List style names applied. Provided styles merged last-win strategy report. Can : single string (recycled), character vector f_combine (recycled), list f_combine results NA sentinels (one--one mapping columns). Use NA list element skip updating labelStyleRef column. isColBreak Whether column triggers page break (length 1 length cols; NA skips position) dedupe Whether deduplicate values (length 1 length cols; NA skips position) blankAfter Whether add blank value change (length 1 length cols; NA skips position) type Data type column format: "string" "numeric" (length 1 length cols; NA skips position). Optional; omit preserve existing. format Format string numeric data (sprintf style), e.g. "%.1f" (length 1 length cols; NA skips position). Optional. missings display missing values columns (length 1 length cols; NA skips position). Optional. colWidth Column width, e.g. "2in", "5cm", "20%" (length 1 length cols; NA skips position — column's width left unchanged). Optional. least one non-NA value specified, affected columns marked LOCKED automatic recalculation remaining unlocked columns triggered autoColWidth = TRUE tfl_set_options(). Locked columns maintain exact width unlocked columns normalize fill remaining available space. valueStyleRef Style names apply cell values. Provided styles merged last-win strategy report. Use NA list element skip updating valueStyleRef column.

title: Define or modify column properties — define_cols
text: Updated spec object

title: Define or modify column properties — define_cols
text: Column Width Management: Widths managed LOCKED/UNLOCKED/VISIBLE partitioning system: VISIBLE columns: included width calculations (isVisible != FALSE) LOCKED columns: exact width specified via colWidth parameter (unit: %, cm, , mm, pt) UNLOCKED columns: automatically recalculated fill available space INVISIBLE columns: hidden output (isVisible = FALSE), assigned width "0.0cm", excluded calculations colWidth specified (visibility changes), columns marked LOCKED remaining UNLOCKED columns automatically recalculated (autoColWidth = TRUE, default): VISIBLE UNLOCKED columns participate recalculation Locked columns (unit) maintain exact specified width Unlocked visible columns normalize proportionally fill remaining available space visible columns' widths sum 100% 1 decimal place precision Invisible columns stay "0.0cm" affect widths disable auto-recalculation, use tfl_set_options(autoColWidth = FALSE). Invisible Column Behavior: isVisible = FALSE automatically sets colWidth = "0.0cm" set colWidth invisible columns (raises error) Invisible columns excluded width recalculation entirely Shown "hidden" flag spec preview

title: Define or modify column properties — define_cols
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data <- data.frame(id = 1:10, age = rnorm(10, 45, 10), group = rep(c("A", "B"), 5))  # Single column with format spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols("age",     label = "Age (years)",     type = "numeric", format = "%.1f", colWidth = "10%"   )  # Batch update with single value spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(c("id", "age", "group"),     isVisible = TRUE  # Applied to all three   )  # Batch update with mapped values spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(c("id", "age"),     label = c("Subject ID", "Age (years)"),  # One-to-one mapping     isID = c(TRUE, FALSE)   )  # Batch format update with mixed recycling spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(c("id", "age"),     type = "numeric",  # Single value recycled to both columns     colWidth = c("10%", "15%")  # Different widths for each column   )  # Multiple calls merge spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols("age",     label = "Age",     type = "numeric", format = "%.0f"   ) |>   define_cols("age",     label = "Age (years)",  # Overrides previous label     colWidth = "15%"  # Merges with format, keeping type and format   )  # Apply style references - single value recycled to all columns spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(c("age", "id"),     labelStyleRef = f_combine("label_style", "emphasis")   )  # Apply different styles combinations to different columns spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(c("id", "age", "group"),     labelStyleRef = c(       f_combine("id_label", "key"),       f_combine("numeric_label", "emphasis"),       f_combine("categorical_label")     )   )  # Column width auto-recalculation (when autoColWidth = TRUE, the default): # Initial widths are automatically distributed (e.g., id=33.3%, age=33.3%, group=33.4%) spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols("id", colWidth = "20%")  # Lock id at 20%   # Result: id=20%, age and group auto-recalculate to fill remaining 80%   #  # Multiple colWidth calls preserve previous locks: spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols("id", colWidth = "20%") |>   define_cols("age", colWidth = "15%")   # Result: id=20% (locked), age=15% (locked), group=65% (fills remaining)  # Disable auto-recalculation to manage widths manually: tfl_set_options(autoColWidth = FALSE)  # Turn off auto-recalculation spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(c("id", "age", "group"), colWidth = c("25%", "30%", "45%"))   # Widths stay exactly as specified, no automatic recalculation tfl_set_options(autoColWidth = TRUE)  # Re-enable (restore default)   # Using tidyselect helpers spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(starts_with("age"),     label = "Age-related metric",     type = "numeric", format = "%.1f"   )  # Using negation with tidyselect spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(-id,  # Exclude id column     isVisible = TRUE   )  # Using column range spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(age:group,  # All columns from age to group     labelStyleRef = "emphasis"   )  # Hide column from output while keeping data for conditional logic spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols("age",     isVisible = FALSE  # Automatically sets width to "0.0cm"   )   # Result: age column hidden, other columns recalculated to sum to 100% } # }

title: ksTFL Package — ksTFL-package

JSON inspection, use save_report() replay_report(). get started, see vignettes: vignette("Getting_Started_with_ksTFL") — Quick start full workflow overview vignette("Styling_Guide_with_ksTFL") — Complete styling reference built-atoms vignette("Reporting_Examples_with_ksTFL") — Progressive real-world examples vignette("Advanced_StyleRows") — Conditional formatting compute_cols() vignette("Column_Width_Management") — Column width locking auto-calculation vignette("Font_Management") — System font discovery, fallbacks, rescanning vignette("Rendering_Pipeline") — Full C++ renderer architecture internals

title: Save TFL Report to JSON with Data Files — save_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ spec1 <- create_table(mtcars) spec2 <- create_text() report <- create_report(spec1, spec2)  # Save with explicit parameters result <- save_report(   report,   docFileName = "report.docx",   outDir = "/output/path",   metaPath = "/meta/path" )  # Save with defaults (uses tempdir and tfl_options) result <- save_report(report, docFileName = "report.docx")  cat("Spec saved as:", result$spec_file, "\n") cat("Saved to:", result$metaPath, "\n")  # Generate a report with an auto-populated TOC page t1 <- create_table(adsl) |>   add_title("Table 1: Demographics", toclevel = 1) |>   set_document(hasData = TRUE)  t2 <- create_table(advs) |>   add_title("Table 2: Vital Signs by Visit", toclevel = 1) |>   add_subtitle("#ByGroup1", toclevel = 2) |>   set_document(hasData = TRUE)  report <- create_report(t1, t2) save_report(   report,   docFileName  = "tables.docx",   outDir       = "output/",   insertTOC    = TRUE,   tocTitle     = "List of Tables" ) # Open tables.docx in Word, click the TOC placeholder, press F9 to populate it. } # }

title: Set document properties — set_document
text: spec TFL spec object isContinues Logical. TRUE, page breaks specs multi-spec report suppressed next spec continues page. contentWidth Width content, e.g. "100%", "25cm", "10in". footnotePlace Character; controls footnotes rendered. One "doc_footer" (place inside Word footer, footer rows), "repeated" (place table every page), "last_page" (place table last page ). Default "repeated". hasData Logical. Whether spec data render. Set TRUE tables data rows. FALSE, body text () rendered instead. topEmptyLine Empty spacer row height table header (table-level), e.g. "6pt". Use NULL disable. "0pt" treated spacer row. bottomEmptyLine Empty spacer row height table bottom border (table-level), e.g. "6pt". Use NULL disable. docTemplate Character. Template use rendering. Accepts either: Name bundled template (see tfl_list_templates()). Full path custom styles JSON file. figureWidth Figure width units, e.g. "6in", "70%", "16.51cm". relevant docType = "Figure". figureHeight Figure height units. syntax figureWidth. figureDevice Character. Image format ggplot2 rendering. One "svg", "png", "jpeg". figureScaleMode Character. figure scaled DOCX. One "fixed" (exact dimensions) "fitWidth" (scale page width, preserving aspect ratio).

title: Update the session package settings — tfl_set_options
text: Update ksTFL session options. function accepts: Named scalar options (e.g. contentWidth = "95%", missings = "."). Settings objects produced helper constructors add_header(), add_footer(), add_body_text(), page objects p_page(). mixture named values settings objects.

title: Update the session package settings — tfl_set_options
code: tfl_set_options(   ...,   docTemplate = NULL,   footnotePlace = NULL,   isContinues = NULL,   contentWidth = NULL,   missings = NULL,   figureWidth = NULL,   figureHeight = NULL,   figureDevice = NULL,   figureScaleMode = NULL,   autoColWidth = NULL,   minColWidth = NULL,   insertTOC = NULL,   tocTitle = NULL,   output_directory = NULL,   meta_directory = NULL )

title: Update the session package settings — tfl_set_options
text: ... Named arguments settings objects returned helper constructors. Named scalar options (e.g. contentWidth = "95%", missings = "."). Settings objects produced helper constructors add_header(), add_footer(), add_style(), add_body_text(), set_page_style(p_page(p_margins())). mixture named values settings objects accepted; function routes appropriate internal slot. docTemplate Character; name predefined bundled template (e.g. "CRO Example_default", "Navy_Pro") file path external template JSON file. NULL (default) current session template left unchanged. Use tfl_reset_options() restore built-default ("CRO Example_default"). footnotePlace Character; controls footnotes rendered. One "doc_footer" (place inside Word footer, footer rows), "repeated" (place table every page), "last_page" (place table last page ). Default "repeated". isContinues Logical; override continuation behavior. contentWidth Character; width content area (e.g. "100%", "95%"). missings Character; default representation missing values (e.g. "NA", ".", "—"). figureWidth Character; default width figure output (e.g. "6in", "16cm"). Applied create_figure() specs specify width. figureHeight Character; default height figure output (e.g. "4in", "10cm"). Applied create_figure() specs specify height. figureDevice Character; graphics device used figure rendering (e.g. "png", "pdf", "svg"). Default depends system capabilities. figureScaleMode Character; figures scaled page content area. Typically "fit" (scale fit) "exact" (use exact dimensions). autoColWidth Logical; enable automatic column width recalculation user sets colWidth via define_cols(). Default TRUE. TRUE, locked columns maintain exact width unlocked columns normalize fill remaining space. Set FALSE disable auto-recalculation manage widths manually. minColWidth Numeric; minimum relative column width (%) unlocked columns recalculation. Default 0.5. Used validate relative widths squeeze columns acceptable minimum. insertTOC Logical; TRUE renderer prepends Table Contents page (using { TOC \f \h \z } field) first spec. Requires least one add_title() add_subtitle() call toclevel set. Default FALSE. Can overridden per-render via save_report(insertTOC = ). tocTitle Character; heading text placed TOC field TOC page. Default "Table Contents". Set "" omit heading. Can overridden per-render via save_report(tocTitle = ). output_directory Character; path default output directory rendered document. meta_directory Character; path default directory intermediate metadata (JSON specs, data, asset files) created rendering.

title: Update the session package settings — tfl_set_options
text: updated settings list, returned invisibly. Use tfl_get_options() inspect.

title: Update the session package settings — tfl_set_options
text: function tries intelligently route supplied object appropriate internal settings slot (headers, footers, styles, bodyText, page).

title: Update the session package settings — tfl_set_options
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Set a named option tfl_set_options(contentWidth = "95%", missings = ".")  # Set a predefined bundled template tfl_set_options(docTemplate = "Navy_Pro")  # Set an external template file tfl_set_options(docTemplate = "/path/to/my_template.json")  # Update page style via helper  tfl_set_options(    set_page_style(page= p_page(    size = "Letter",    orientation = "portrait",    margins = p_margins(top = "1in", bottom = "1in", left = "0.75in", right = "0.75in")  )))  # Add default header and footer via helpers tfl_set_options(   add_header(c("Left Header", "Center Header", "Right Header")),   add_footer(c("Left Footer", "Center Footer", "Right Footer")) )  # Add default body text via helper tfl_set_options(   add_body_text("This is the default body text for all text specs.") )  # Control automatic column width recalculation # Enable auto-recalculation (default): tfl_set_options(autoColWidth = TRUE) spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols("id", colWidth = "20%")  # Locks id, others auto-adjust  # Disable auto-recalculation for manual width management: tfl_set_options(autoColWidth = FALSE) spec <- create_table(data) |>   define_cols(c("id", "age"), colWidth = c("20%", "30%"))  # Exact widths, no auto-adjust  # Enable TOC page for all reports in the session tfl_set_options(insertTOC = TRUE, tocTitle = "List of Tables") # Then mark individual titles/subtitles with toclevel: spec <- create_table(adsl) |>   add_title("Table 1: Demographics", toclevel = 1) # save_report() will now prepend a TOC page automatically. # In Word: click the TOC placeholder and press F9 to populate it. } # }

title: Save and Render a TFL Report to DOCX — write_doc
code: write_doc(   report,   name,   outDir = tfl_get_option("output_directory"),   metaPath = tfl_get_option("meta_directory") %||% tempdir(),   prettify = FALSE,   toc = tfl_get_option("insertTOC"),   tocTitle = tfl_get_option("tocTitle"),   overrideTemplate = NULL,   font_dirs = NULL,   fallback_font = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Save and Render a TFL Report to DOCX — write_doc
text: report TFL_report object created create_report(). name Character(1). Base file name (without extension) output DOCX document. .docx extension appended automatically. outDir Character(1). Directory final DOCX file written. Defaults tfl_get_option("output_directory"). metaPath Character(1). Directory intermediate specification JSON associated data/figure files stored. Defaults tfl_get_option("meta_directory"). prettify Logical. TRUE, pretty‑prints JSON written save_report() easier inspection. Default FALSE (compact JSON). toc Logical. TRUE, enables automatic insertion Table Contents page via save_report(). Defaults tfl_get_option("insertTOC"). tocTitle Character(1). Heading placed TOC field TOC page. Defaults tfl_get_option("tocTitle"). overrideTemplate Optional character string. Global template override used internal renderer specs. Accepts either: predefined bundled template name (e.g. "Navy_Pro"). file path (absolute relative) external template JSON file. NULL (default), templates resolved per-spec spec's docTemplate value (allowing mixed templates multi-spec reports). provided name/path resolved, warning emitted CRO Example_default used. font_dirs Optional character vector additional directories search fonts rendering. fallback_font Optional character string. Path fallback font file used renderer. NULL, package default used. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints renderer progress messages.

title: Save and Render a TFL Report to DOCX — write_doc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic end-to-end workflow ---------------------------------------------- library(ksTFL)  # Create a simple table spec tbl <- create_table(mtcars) |>   add_title("Table 1: Motor Trend Car Road Tests") |>   set_document(hasData = TRUE)  # Combine into a report rpt <- create_report(tbl)  # Write DOCX to the default output directory (getwd() by default) doc_path <- write_doc(   report = rpt,   name   = "mtcars_demo" )  cat("DOCX written to:", doc_path, "\n")  # Custom output and meta directories, with TOC --------------------------- tfl_set_options(   output_directory = "output",   insertTOC = TRUE,   tocTitle  = "List of Tables" )  rpt2 <- create_report(tbl) write_doc(   report   = rpt2,   name     = "mtcars_with_toc",   outDir   = "output",   metaPath = file.path(tempdir(), "ksTFL_meta"),   prettify = TRUE,   toc      = TRUE ) } # }

title: ksTFL 0.8.0
text: Page settings (p_page() / set_page_style()) now support partial overrides — explicitly specified fields override template defaults, enabling lighter per-spec customisation. Per-column style mapping: character vector style_refs add_style() now accepts vector length equal number columns, NA skip individual columns. New Listings_times built-template (Times New Roman variant Listings template).

title: ksTFL 0.8.0
text: add_header() / add_footer() level replacement now correctly returns right TFL_options_header / TFL_options_footer class used via tfl_set_options(). Default page settings changed fixed A4 / portrait override NULL, bare specs inherit page properties active template without unintended overrides.

title: ksTFL 0.7.9

GitHub Releases now published private public (ksTFL-release) repositories. New manual workflow (“Upload Binary Release Repo”) adding locally built packages (e.g., macOS) release repository. Added explicit pandoc setup ensure vignettes build correctly platforms. Updated README installation instructions per-platform sections.

title: ksTFL 0.7.6
text: produces real paragraph boundaries emitted DOCX text groups (titles, subtitles, body text, footnotes), aligned pagination measurement logic. Improved spacer-row border behavior: left/right borders preserved top/bottom empty spacer rows, top/bottom lines remain suppressed intended.

Improved code comments narrative descriptions across example vignettes. Translated example scripts (lb_lst_01_en.R, ae_tbl_exmpl.R, tmp_example.R) Russian English.

title: ksTFL 0.6.0
text: Arial → Liberation Sans (bundled) Times New Roman → Liberation Serif (bundled) Courier New → Liberation Mono (bundled) Georgia → Liberation Serif (bundled) Verdana → Liberation Sans (bundled) Trebuchet MS → Liberation Sans (bundled) Aptos font family dropped. Aptos longer target font. Existing specs referencing Aptos fall back Liberation Sans. hardcoded font_map C++ font cache removed. Font resolution now fully dynamic.

title: ksTFL 0.6.0
text: Runtime font discovery. package load (.onLoad()), C++ font scanner inspects system font directories (platform-specific) builds global font registry. System-installed fonts always preferred bundled fallbacks. tfl_rescan_fonts() — Re-run font discovery installing new fonts changing ksTFL.font_dirs. Prints resolution report console. tfl_font_status() — Print current font resolution report without rescanning. ksTFL.font_dirs option — Point options(ksTFL.font_dirs = c("/path//fonts")) additional directories containing proprietary custom fonts. directories scanned alongside system directories. Startup font report. package attached, message reports target fonts use fallback, guidance run tfl_font_status() details.

title: ksTFL 0.6.0
text: New C++ module: font_scanner.h / font_scanner.cpp — platform-specific font directory enumeration (Windows registry, macOS standard dirs, Linux XDG/freedesktop dirs) FreeType-based family/style classification. font_cache.cpp now resolves fonts via scanner’s global path map, two-tier fallback chain: designated fallback family (e.g. Georgia → Liberation Serif), Liberation Sans last resort. rcpp_bindings.cpp exports init_font_registry_impl() get_font_dirs_impl() R. render_docx.R now collects font directories scanner cache via get_font_dirs_impl() instead using bundled inst/fonts/ directory.
